title: Mary Douglas on Purity and Danger: An Interview
author: Featherstone and Turner

opening_move.description: A previously unpublished 1987 interview with Mary Douglas is introduced and contextualized: it captures Douglas at mid-career, shortly after Purity and Danger became a classic, and reveals her intellectual formation, her relationship with Evans-Pritchard, and her reluctance to engage with social constructivist and deconstructivist framings of her work.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: The document is of intellectual-historical value: it shows Douglas's own account of her work, her Catholic intellectual formation, and the tensions between her anthropological approach and the sociological/constructivist agenda of her interviewers.

theoretical_positioning.description: The paper is a document publication with contextual introduction. Featherstone and Turner frame the interview historically, noting the tensions between Douglas as anthropologist and themselves as sociologists interested in the body and social constructivism.

epistemic_mode.qualitative_methods: Interview transcript — a primary archival document (1987 interview, published for first time in 2022); framed by biographical and intellectual-historical contextualization.
data_sources: 1987 interview with Mary Douglas at Lancaster University
epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: Intellectual biography and hermeneutic contextualization of a primary historical document.
epistemic_mode.notes: This is an unusual format: primarily a document publication with contextual framing. The interview itself is the empirical material. The epistemic mode is best characterized as purely_interpretive_hermeneutic since the paper's main function is to present and contextualize a historical document rather than advance a normative argument.

confidence_notes: This paper does not fit neatly into the schema — it is primarily a document publication (interview transcript) with scholarly framing. The epistemic mode is purely_interpretive_hermeneutic because there is no normative argument: the paper's function is archival/historical. The normative_stake is absent/low. The move_sequence reflects the introduction's structure: contextualize the document's significance → situate Douglas's career → present the intellectual-historical framing → the interview follows.
